methodsAttempted.query: site:tilburguniversity.edu Tilburg University generative AI policy ChatGPT artificial intelligence students exam academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search surfaced the official TSHD fraud, plagiarism, and AI use page; the Tilburg University privacy/security GenAI guidance page; a TiSEM assessment PDF; a research PDF; and a research-showcase publication.

methodsAttempted.query: site:tilburguniversity.edu Tilburg University AI tools generative AI privacy data ChatGPT policy
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search ranked the official Privacy & Security framework for responsible use of GenAI and the related working-responsibly guidance page first.

Firecrawl search surfaced an official student skills page, news, project, research, and event pages. These were treated as lower-specificity context unless they contained direct policy or guidance text.
Verified that the used sources are under Tilburg University's official public domain and are not disallowed by the public robots.txt rules for the fetched paths.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found Tilburg University privacy/security guidance for responsible GenAI use and a school-specific TSHD student academic-integrity page on AI use in exams and assignments. Claims are scoped to those official sources; broader student-skills, news, and research/publication pages were not generalized into university-wide policy.

artifacts.claimText: Tilburg University's Privacy & Security framework says users should not enter personal data, confidential information, or commercially sensitive information into GenAI tools, and should have tools assessed first if they want to process sensitive data with GenAI.

citation.citationTitle: Privacy & Security framework for responsible use of GenAI
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Privacy & Security framework for responsible use of GenAI
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Do not enter personal data, confidential, or commercially sensitive information into GenAI tools. These may be stored or processed by third parties and often do not comply with privacy legislation. This also applies when risk-mitigating features are enabled. Do you want to process sensitive data with GenAI? Then have the tool assessed by your information manager first.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Key guidelines: Only use public data

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: GenAI may contain errors, fabrications, or biases. It is not a search engine that you can use to retrieve or verify facts. Always verify the generated information before using or sharing it. Ultimately, you remain responsible for what happens with the output.

artifacts.title: Tilburg University AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Tilburg University has official privacy and security guidance for responsible GenAI use from its CISO Office, including limits on personal, confidential, commercially sensitive, and research data; verification of GenAI output; adherence to privacy and information-security policies; and reporting accidental sharing of confidential or personal information. A separate official page for Tilburg School of Humanities and Digital Sciences gives school-specific academic-integrity guidance on AI use in exams, tests, and assignments. This staged bundle keeps TSHD claims scoped to that school and rejects lower-specificity skills, news, and research-showcase pages.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The TSHD academic-integrity claims are school-specific and should not be generalized to all Tilburg University schools without additional official evidence.
News, research-showcase, event, and skills/tutorial pages were not used as policy evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
